claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few questions since he's 17 years old.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he attending school, college, studying for A-levels, or doing an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He left school and isn't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a follow-up question:

**Has Alex registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services after leaving education?** (If so, this would need to have happened within the last 20 weeks)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't registered with any careers service or anything like that. He just left school and hasn't signed up for any of those services.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one more important question:

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at the moment. He's just at home with me right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the situation based on the rules:

- Alex is 17 years old
- He is not in approved education or training
- He has not registered with a government-sponsored careers service or armed services
- He is not working

According to the Child Benefit rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 **only if** they stay in approved education or training. There is an exception allowing Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services.

Since Alex is not in approved education or training, and has not registered with a careers service or armed services, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete: Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

However, if Alex's circumstances change in the future - for example, if he:
- Returns to approved education or training
- Registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services

Then you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit for him again. In that case, you would need to make a new claim or notify HMRC of the change in circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which is over the standard age limit of 16. Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 only if they remain in approved education or training. Alex is not currently in any education or training. There is an exception that allows Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, but Alex has not registered with either of these services. Therefore, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old. While they would normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16 living with them, Alex has exceeded the standard age limit. The extended eligibility for 16-19 year olds requires the child to be in approved education or training, which Alex is not. Alex also has not registered with a careers service or armed services that would allow the 20-week extension. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old, which is over the standard age limit of 16. Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 only if they remain in approved education or training. Alex is not currently in any education or training. There is an exception that allows Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, but Alex has not registered with either of these services. Therefore, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old. While they would normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16 living with them, Alex has exceeded the standard age limit. The extended eligibility for 16-19 year olds requires the child to be in approved education or training, which Alex is not. Alex also has not registered with a careers service or armed services that would allow the 20-week extension. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
